mechanism_narrative: An emergency federal funds rate cut of more than 100 basis points within a single month signals aggressive central bank crisis response, lowering the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows and triggering a short-term relief rally in the S&P 500 over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS drops more than 100bp within a 1-month window
evidence_refs: fact:Fed cut rates to near-zero in emergency action around March 2020
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following the trigger are negative or not significantly different from unconditional baseline (t-test p > 0.05) across at least two historical emergency-cut episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Persistently low unemployment reported with a one-month lag reflects a labor market that had not yet absorbed a demand shock, so continued low UNRATE readings act as a lagging but stable trend signal supporting equity prices over a quarterly horizon until the labor data catches up to the shock.
failure_condition: Rank IC between UNRATE level and 60-day forward returns falls below 0.03 over any rolling 3-year window, or returns turn negative despite stable low UNRATE readings during known shock periods.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in retail sales exceeding 2% signals an abrupt contraction in consumer demand, which forward-predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as earnings expectations for consumer-facing sectors are revised downward.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS snapshot as of t-2mo shows 735904.0 million dollars, pre-shock level
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day forward returns in more than 55% of instances following a >2% RSAFS decline, or Rank IC stays below 0.03 across rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A year-over-year decline in industrial production signals broad-based supply-side contraction, which forward-predicts negative S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon as industrial and manufacturing earnings deteriorate.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following negative INDPRO YoY readings are not significantly below unconditional baseline (t-test p > 0.05) over at least two historical contraction episodes.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Reserve cuts rates sharply while unemployment simultaneously begins rising, the joint signal indicates policy is front-loading stimulus in anticipation of a labor market deterioration not yet reflected in lagging data, producing a short-horizon positive equity reaction as markets price in policy support outpacing the deteriorating fundamentals.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS falls more than 50bp in the same month UNRATE rises
evidence_refs: fact:emergency rate cuts coincided with anticipated labor deterioration in March 2020
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following joint FEDFUNDS cut and UNRATE rise are negative in more than 55% of instances, or interaction term shows no incremental predictive power over single-series models (delta Rank IC < 0.01).

mechanism_narrative: Declines in housing starts are a powerful negative predictor of forward equity returns specifically during recession or crisis regimes, because housing is a leading indicator of broader demand collapse, whereas in expansion regimes small HOUST fluctuations carry little forward information for equities.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines more than 10% from 3-month prior reading
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST snapshot at 1246.0 thousand units as of t-3mo, pre-shock baseline
failure_condition: In identified recession regimes, 20-day forward returns following a >10% HOUST decline are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05), or the effect is equally strong in expansion regimes, invalidating the regime-conditional claim.

mechanism_narrative: Stable, low consumer price inflation removes pressure on the central bank to tighten policy, providing a persistent tailwind for equity valuations via sustained low discount rates over a short 5-day horizon as markets continuously price in accommodative conditions.
failure_condition: Rank IC between CPIAUCSL stability and 5-day forward returns falls below 0.02 over any rolling 1-year window, or returns are negative in more than 55% of low-inflation periods.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous contraction in real GDP and a decline in nonfarm payroll employment jointly confirm an economy entering recession, and this dual confirmation predicts sustained negative S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon as corporate earnings across the economy are revised down in tandem with labor shedding.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 snapshot 24024.957 billion at t-6mo, PAYEMS 159526 thousand at t-1mo predate the COVID shock and are subject to large revision
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following joint GDPC1 contraction and PAYEMS decline are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05), or the joint signal underperforms either single-series signal alone in out-of-sample Rank IC.
